topic_scope.headline: Why LLMs output a probability distribution, not a single fixed answer
topic_scope.detail: All sub-concepts (token, vocabulary, softmax, sampling, stochastic vs deterministic, generation loop) are directly necessary to answer the headline question. Softmax is explained in plain language without the formula — appropriate depth. Temperature and neural network architecture are named-and-deferred in ≤1 sentence each. No RLHF, fine-tuning, transformer architecture details, or attention mechanism content found.

findings.note: Used in sections 5.1 and 8 without plain-language definition on first use. Minor risk given context but worth a one-line gloss.

terms_defined_on_first_use.notes: LLM expanded at first use (section 4). Deterministic, stochastic, token, vocabulary, softmax, sampling, probability distribution all defined on first use in their respective sections.

jargon_dumps.notes: No paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms simultaneously. Section 5.1 introduces token + vocabulary (2 terms). Section 5.3 introduces softmax + raw scores (2 terms).

plain_register.notes: Conversational tone throughout. Average sentence length well under 25 words. No excessive hedging phrases detected.
